open_hooks_to_carry: Make the deposition precise about mechanisms, not just moral outrage.
Show the concession's loss as meaningful but partial.
prev_tail_excerpt:
...front. Gabriel reached for the receipt, flipped it over, and wrote a number on the back in his narrow exact hand: not an escape address, not a code, just the inquiry contact line from the folder, copied so he would not have to unfold the official paper in the rain or in front of the wrong person. Then he slid the receipt into the clear sleeve with the rest of his release conditions.

"I'm not staying because I believe in hearings," he said.

"I didn't think you were."

"Good." He tapped the folder once. "I'm staying because if I run, they get to turn all this back into paperwork about damaged equipment and unauthorized conduct. I don't want their clean version. I want the room to stink when they tell it."

Marisol looked at the municipal seal ghosting blue through the plastic, at his thumb holding it flat, at the cheap bakery steam fading off the table between them. Witness was not safety. It was another kind of custody, entered on purpose. It would ask for his body again, for her body again, for the exact language they had spent a summer trying to keep from being buried. He knew that. He tucked the folder under his arm anyway, the same careful motion he might have used for a borrowed tape or a list of names that could not be trusted to memory alone.

Outside, the buses kept arriving. Nobody cheered. Nobody was saved by soup. But when Gabriel stood, he waited for her to gather her bag before turning toward the door, and the pause was so small and so familiar that Marisol felt it where she used to feel his night breathing through the bed slats: a damaged rhythm, still there.
state_deltas_required: Formal inquiry, legal self-protection, and the knowledge that institutions mutate even when one concession falls.
Marisol turns sabotage into witness by naming exactly how the trains were maintained and broken, while the line is stopped but not historically undone.
